items.title: AI SDK 7
items.verdict_reason: Duplicate of changelog entry (2d80d9ec); same story, preferring changelog version
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Vercel published a blog post about AI SDK 7, covering the same content as the changelog entry. Preferring the changelog version.

items.raw_claim: Vercel built a product-design skill file system that gives coding agents access to product design rationale alongside code
items.summary: Vercel describes their system for encoding product design decisions as agent-accessible context in the repo via a product-design skill directory. Agents understand why design patterns exist (not just what shipped). The SKILL.md routes tasks by mode (shape/implement/review/copy/harden) and points to canonical sources for component APIs, design-system rules, and interaction guidance. Low-cost pattern any AI-assisted builder can adapt.
items.title: Teaching agents product design at Vercel
items.verdict_reason: Useful agent workflow pattern from a production team; worth reading for AI-assisted builders
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Directly applicable to any builder doing AI-assisted development; low-cost, high-leverage pattern

items.raw_claim: Vercel released AI SDK 7 with breaking changes, unified agent harnesses, file/skill uploads, and MCP Apps support
items.summary: AI SDK 7 is a major release of the most-used TypeScript AI/agent SDK (16M weekly downloads). Breaking changes require migration via v7 codemods. Key additions: agent harness adapters for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Deep Agents behind a unified interface; standardized reasoning control across all providers; typed tool context for 3rd-party tools; file/skill upload APIs so large inputs are uploaded once and referenced by pointer; MCP Apps for server-rendered app UIs. Also expands realtime, video, transcription, and structured output. Read the migration guide before your next deploy.
items.title: AI SDK 7 is now available
items.uncertainty: Breaking changes not yet validated by 3rd-party builders in production
items.verdict_reason: Major release with breaking changes for the most-downloaded TypeScript AI SDK; agent harnesses + MCP Apps change how builders integrate AI
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Major version release with breaking changes for the most-downloaded TypeScript AI SDK

items.raw_claim: Deep Agents and OpenCode adapters are now available in the AI SDK Harness
items.summary: Vercel added Deep Agents and OpenCode adapters to the AI SDK Harness, enabling these coding agent runtimes inside Vercel Sandboxes. Sub-announcement within AI SDK 7 release.
items.title: Deep Agents and OpenCode are now available in the AI SDK Harness
items.verdict_reason: Covered by AI SDK 7 announcement; sub-feature of same release
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Vercel added Deep Agents and OpenCode adapters to the AI SDK Harness, enabling these coding agent runtimes inside Vercel Sandboxes. Sub-announcement within AI SDK 7 release.

items.worth_mentioning_reason: Vercel Pro teams can now run up to 500 concurrent builds (up from 12), billed by build minutes used.

items.raw_claim: The Vesuvius Challenge successfully read an entire Herculaneum scroll using ML/vision models
items.summary: The Vesuvius Challenge achieved its goal: ML/vision models and human expertise decoded an entire charred scroll from ancient Herculaneum for the first time in ~2,000 years. Preprint available at scrollprize.org/pdf/main.pdf. Significant milestone for AI applied to physical artifact restoration.
items.title: An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time
items.verdict_reason: Impressive ML milestone worth knowing; not directly actionable but historically significant
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Significant ML milestone; demonstrates AI applied to physical artifact analysis

items.raw_claim: A builder exposed their AI legal assistant to 2,000+ people and documented real-world prompt injection and jailbreaking patterns
items.summary: Fernando Iazeolla built a public AI legal assistant (HackMyClaw) and invited 2,000+ people to try to break it. Documents real attack patterns, success rates, defense mechanisms, and lessons. First-hand empirical security research from a solo builder. If you ship any AI tool with user-controlled input, prompt injection and jailbreaking patterns from real attackers are directly applicable.
items.title: What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant
items.uncertainty: Content not directly read; summary based on title and community context
items.verdict_reason: Direct empirical security research from a solo builder; actionable for anyone building AI tools with user input
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Real security intelligence from a solo builder; actionable for anyone building AI tools with user input

No immediate action, but worth tracking for web product operators — requirements could materialize within 1-2 years in key markets.
items.title: The 'papers, please' era of the internet will decimate your privacy

items.verdict_reason: Regulatory risk in motion; not immediate but worth tracking for web product operators
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Regulatory risk worth tracking for web product operators

private_memo:
# Morning memo — 2026-06-27

**Source failures:** None observed.

## Worth attention

- **AI SDK 7 is now available** — https://vercel.com/changelog/ai-sdk-7
  Vercel released AI SDK 7, a major TypeScript AI/agent SDK update (16M weekly downloads). Breaking changes require migration. Key additions: agent harness adapters to run Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Deep Agents behind a unified interface; standardized reasoning control across providers; typed tool context for 3rd-party tools; file/skill upload APIs to avoid re-sending large blobs per call; MCP Apps support for mixing model-visible and app-only tools. Run the v7 codemods before your next deploy.

- **What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant** — https://www.fernandoi.cl/posts/hackmyclaw/
  A builder exposed their AI legal assistant to 2,000+ people with an open invitation to break it. Real-world findings on prompt injection, jailbreaking, and boundary testing in production AI apps. If you ship any AI tool with user-controlled input, this is directly relevant security intelligence.

- **Teaching agents product design at Vercel** — https://vercel.com/blog/teaching-agents-product-design-at-vercel
  Vercel describes how they encode product design decisions as agent-accessible context in the repo using a product-design skill file. Agents understand why components exist, not just what shipped. Low-cost pattern applicable to any AI-assisted codebase.

- **Vibecoding gets Emacs patch rejected** — https://xlii.space/eng/honesty-gets-emacs-patch-rejected/
  A developer's AI-generated Emacs patch was rejected after they disclosed it was AI-written. Established open source communities are forming norms against AI-generated patches. Worth knowing before your next open source contribution.

- **An entire Herculaneum scroll has been read for the first time** — https://scrollprize.org/firstscroll
  Vesuvius Challenge succeeded: ML/vision models plus human expertise decoded an entire carbonized ancient scroll for the first time in ~2,000 years.

- **The papers-please era of the internet will decimate your privacy** — https://expression.fire.org/p/the-papers-please-era-of-the-internet
  Age verification laws requiring government ID for internet access are spreading globally. Regulatory risk for web product operators; could become mandatory compliance within 1-2 years in key markets.

- **The Exhaustion of Talking to a Tool** — https://ohadravid.github.io/posts/2026-06-tool-talking/
  Essay on the cognitive and emotional toll of heavy AI tool use. For builders using AI tools daily, a reflection on sustainable usage patterns.

## Full digest
- [P] [vercel-changelog] AI SDK 7 is now available — https://vercel.com/changelog/ai-sdk-7 — Major TypeScript AI SDK release with agent harnesses, file/skill uploads, MCP Apps; breaking changes
- [P] [hn-top] What happened after 2k people tried to hack my AI assistant — https://www.fernandoi.cl/posts/hackmyclaw/ — Real security learnings from exposing AI assistant to 2k attackers
- [P] [vercel-changelog] Teaching agents product design at Vercel — https://vercel.com/blog/teaching-agents-product-design-at-vercel — System for encoding product design decisions as agent-accessible repo context
- [P] [lobsters] Vibecoding gets Emacs patch rejected — https://xlii.space/eng/honesty-gets-emacs-patch-rejected/ — AI code disclosure triggered patch rejection; open source community norms forming
- [P] [hn-top] An entire Herculaneum scroll read for first time — https://scrollprize.org/firstscroll — ML/vision decoded full ancient scroll; Vesuvius Challenge milestone
- [P] [lobsters] The Exhaustion of Talking to a Tool — https://ohadravid.github.io/posts/2026-06-tool-talking/ — Essay on AI tool fatigue
- [M] [hn-top] The papers-please era of the internet — https://expression.fire.org/p/the-papers-please-era-of-the-internet — Age verification laws spreading; regulatory risk for web products
- [R] [google-ai-changelog] Interactions API — no content to assess
- [R] [vercel-changelog] AI SDK 7 blog post — duplicate of changelog entry
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Deep Agents and OpenCode harness — covered by AI SDK 7 announcement
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Pro teams 500 concurrent builds — large-team feature, not solo-dev relevant
- [R] [vercel-changelog] Preserve local env vars CLI — minor UX fix
- [R] [lobsters] font-family recommendations — reference, not news
- [R] [lobsters] Om Malik, 1966-2026 — obituary; not work-relevant; duplicate
- [R] [lobsters] New @bitCast Semantics Zig — niche Zig update
- [R] [lobsters] Oxide Rack 3D Explorer — hardware showcase
- [R] [hn-top] Om Malik has died — duplicate obituary
- [R] [hn-top] Libre Barcode Project — niche font utility
- [R] [hn-top] Framework 10G Ethernet module — hardware deep dive
- [R] [deferred x21] Various — deferred by pre-triage cap, not scored this run
